title: Expose the canonical operator entry as a typed Grabowski surface
goal: Let a fresh ChatGPT operator read the canonical heim-pc operator entry, its content hash and resolved local locators through one typed read-only Grabowski call, then select the correct primary truth source without relying on conversation memory or broad filesystem discovery.

acceptance.assertion: One typed read-only surface returns schema version, canonical source path, source SHA-256, resolved host paths, operator role, human role, entry sequence, truth-source locators, safety boundaries and explicit non-claims.
A deterministic classifier distinguishes single-repository, cross-repository, host-runtime, task-selection and historical-evidence work and returns only the required next read surfaces.
The response detects missing, malformed or source/projection hash drift and never substitutes embedded prose for an unreadable canonical contract.
Path resolution is bounded to declared templates and cannot trigger a broad home scan or expose secret, browser-profile, keyring or private-content paths.
Tests cover valid entry, missing source, malformed JSON, source/projection drift, unresolved HOME, unsupported schema, scope routing and privacy boundaries.
The surface grants no mutation, task, merge, deploy or secret-read authority and claims no live health beyond freshly read primary sources.

evidence.current_gap: Grabowski bootstrap and embedded agent instructions do not expose heim-pc or systemkatalog as a first-class typed route.
